sections.paragraph:
Αἰνέας, ου, ὁ (lit.; ins [e.g. ISyriaW 1929; 2238, also fr. Palestine: SEG VIII, 255–112/111 b.c.]; pap [Preisigke, Namenbuch]) Aeneas at Lydia cured of palsy Ac 9:33f.—DDD s.v. Aeneas.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

ISyriaW ISyriaW = Inscriptions grecques et latines de la Syrie, ed. WWaddington, continuation of LWB—List 3

  1. fr. = from

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Preisigke, Preisigke, Namenbuch = Namenbuch 1922

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 27
